notes_for_human_reviewer: Classic novice: reads requirements at face value ('the requirements look comprehensive'), praises rather than analyses, uses LinkedIn-speak ('leverage best practices', 'good stakeholder engagement'), and self-suppresses ('I do not want to step on her toes'). The 'I assume the minutes are somewhere' on REQ-4.1.9 actually exhibits the failure mode the chapter warns against (treating unstated assumptions as fine). Only one weak surfacing move (vague 'meeting with Priya'). No requirement IDs quoted in failure-mode terms, no downstream artefacts named, no traceability reasoning. Role posture caps at 2 because self-suppression is explicit.
